buyer_briefs.buyer_trigger: Needs enterprises to trust agents that retrieve context, connect custom MCP servers, delegate work, and execute write actions under explicit governance.
buyer_briefs.customer_evidence_needed: Design-partner telemetry for production MCP calls, write-action confirmations, signed receipts, and renewal intent.
buyer_briefs.likely_objection: The repo is not yet a hosted enforcement plane.
buyer_briefs.paid_wedge: Hosted MCP policy, connector trust monitoring, signed context releases, run receipt vault, and enterprise trust-center exports.
buyer_briefs.primary_decision: Acquire, partner, or integrate a vendor-neutral secure context and MCP evidence layer.
buyer_briefs.proof_path: Start with the trust-center export, show the protocol conformance pack, then use the value model to separate open distribution from hosted MCP enforcement.
buyer_briefs.short_pitch: SecurityRecipes gives the lab a source-backed control layer for context, MCP authorization, A2A handoffs, telemetry, receipts, and buyer evidence without closing the open knowledge base.
buyer_briefs.title: Frontier model lab

buyer_briefs.buyer_trigger: Needs platform-wide approval paths for custom agents, MCP servers, context registries, tool-surface drift, and customer-specific evidence.
buyer_briefs.customer_evidence_needed: Customer-private context hashes, live identity-provider bindings, connector metadata, and source-host review records.
buyer_briefs.likely_objection: Needs tenant isolation, live connector inventory, and source-host integrations.
buyer_briefs.paid_wedge: Private context registry, policy drift API, connector inventory ingestion, and tenant trust-center export.
buyer_briefs.primary_decision: Embed SecurityRecipes as the governance packet and policy vocabulary around an existing agent platform.
buyer_briefs.proof_path: Use app intake and posture snapshots for launch review, then attach MCP authorization conformance to protected remote tool access.
buyer_briefs.short_pitch: SecurityRecipes packages the intake, posture, authorization, and source-freshness evidence that platform teams need before approving agent rollouts.
buyer_briefs.title: AI platform vendor

buyer_briefs.buyer_trigger: Needs agentic AI and MCP coverage for AppSec, CNAPP, identity security, SOC, SOAR, or exposure-management portfolios.
buyer_briefs.customer_evidence_needed: Validated integrations with SIEM, ticketing, source-host, identity provider, and approval systems.
buyer_briefs.likely_objection: Existing security platforms already own logs, tickets, identity, and code-host integrations.
buyer_briefs.paid_wedge: Agentic evidence adapter, receipt export, SIEM/SOAR event mapping, and continuous eval replay.
buyer_briefs.primary_decision: Use SecurityRecipes as the agentic control taxonomy and generated evidence layer behind a security product module.
buyer_briefs.proof_path: Lead with standards crosswalk, telemetry contract, and run receipts, then show how trust-center export answers customer questionnaires.
buyer_briefs.short_pitch: The project turns agentic security from narrative guidance into evidence packs, deterministic evaluators, receipts, and OpenTelemetry-aligned fields.
buyer_briefs.title: Security platform vendor

buyer_briefs.buyer_trigger: Needs to approve agent pilots without giving agents broad human authority, unmanaged private context, or unaudited MCP tools.
buyer_briefs.customer_evidence_needed: DPA/retention decisions, tenant-specific policy maps, approval records, and audit-ready run samples.
buyer_briefs.likely_objection: Internal data, compliance, privacy, and audit teams require private deployment proof.
buyer_briefs.paid_wedge: Private deployment, tenant-side context signing, metadata-only telemetry, and trust-center report export.
buyer_briefs.primary_decision: Run a bounded design-partner pilot with explicit evidence collection and safe stop conditions.
buyer_briefs.proof_path: Use the pilot pack to pick one workflow, bind telemetry requirements, run read-only MCP first, and advance to controlled actions only with receipts.
buyer_briefs.short_pitch: SecurityRecipes gives governance, security, and engineering a clear pilot path: trusted context, policy-bound MCP, receipts, telemetry, and holds when evidence is missing.
buyer_briefs.title: Regulated enterprise

buyer_briefs.buyer_trigger: Needs to decide whether this is a defensible product wedge or a well-written documentation site.
buyer_briefs.customer_evidence_needed: Three to five design partners, usage telemetry, renewal signal, hosted auth, tenant isolation, metering, and support motion.
buyer_briefs.likely_objection: There is no recurring revenue or hosted product proof yet.
buyer_briefs.paid_wedge: Hosted MCP policy, private context registry, run receipt vault, connector drift monitoring, and trust-center API.
buyer_briefs.primary_decision: Evaluate whether open evidence plus hosted MCP controls can become a strategic $10-20M acquisition target.
buyer_briefs.proof_path: Show the value model, pilot pack, and trust-center export; then make the missing proof points explicit instead of overclaiming current revenue.
buyer_briefs.short_pitch: SecurityRecipes owns a timely control point between agents, context, MCP tools, identity, telemetry, evals, and buyer evidence, with clear open-core and hosted-product expansion paths.
buyer_briefs.title: VC or acquirer

deal_room_next_steps.function: Replace acquisition assumptions with customer evidence.
deal_room_next_steps.proof_to_collect: Usage, reviewer time, avoided remediation hours, safe holds, false positives, renewal intent, integration requests, and support burden.
deal_room_next_steps.stop_condition: Stop expanding scope if pilots cannot produce approved receipts or measurable buyer pull within 90 days.
deal_room_next_steps.title: Secure three to five design partners

deal_room_next_steps.function: Move from open reference MCP to production control-plane wedge.
deal_room_next_steps.proof_to_collect: Tenant ids, resource indicators, audience validation, session binding, scoped tokens, audit logs, and rate limits.
deal_room_next_steps.stop_condition: Do not support write actions until auth, approval receipts, telemetry, and kill signals are enforced.
deal_room_next_steps.title: Implement hosted MCP auth and tenant isolation

deal_room_next_steps.function: Let customers keep source content private while exporting hashes and retrieval policy evidence.
deal_room_next_steps.proof_to_collect: Source hashes, owners, trust tiers, poisoning scan summaries, attestation status, retention class, and citation policy.
deal_room_next_steps.stop_condition: Do not persist raw prompts, secrets, private code, or tenant content in shared logs.
deal_room_next_steps.title: Add private context registry ingestion

deal_room_next_steps.function: Turn buyer diligence into a repeatable enterprise artifact.
deal_room_next_steps.proof_to_collect: Question coverage, pack freshness, MCP tool availability, source hashes, customer evidence bindings, and redaction state.
deal_room_next_steps.stop_condition: Do not claim trust-center readiness if required packs are stale, missing, or customer runtime proof is unavailable.
deal_room_next_steps.title: Ship trust-center export API

buyer_success_criteria: A reviewer can understand the product thesis in under five minutes without reading every evidence page.
Every important claim points to a generated JSON artifact and an MCP tool.
Assumption-based business claims are separated from reference evidence and future customer runtime proof.
Objections are answered with current agentic AI, MCP, A2A, telemetry, and secure-by-design context.
The next proof points for a credible $10-20M outcome are explicit and measurable.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Owns a strategic layer between agents, tools, private context, and evidence.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Reference evidence and local MCP access are present; hosted enforcement remains the commercial expansion path.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Hosted tenant deployment, live policy decisions, and customer-specific context ingestion.
enterprise_questions.question: What is SecurityRecipes in one enterprise sentence?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: It is the secure context layer for agentic AI: a source-backed, MCP-readable control package for trusted context, tool authorization, agent identity, telemetry, receipts, evals, and buyer evidence.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: The standards surface is moving enough to create recurring demand.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The open site tracks primary sources and maps them to generated controls.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Customer-specific standards drift alerts and internal policy mapping.
enterprise_questions.question: Why is this urgent in 2026?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: MCP, A2A, custom MCP apps, agentic skills, non-human identity, and agent telemetry are moving into enterprise workflows faster than review teams can manually govern them.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Open knowledge becomes distribution while generated evidence becomes the product surface.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Evidence artifacts are real and inspectable; hosted product telemetry is still required for commercial proof.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Runtime logs, receipts, customer-private evidence, and paid pilot outcomes.
enterprise_questions.question: Why is this more than a documentation library?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The repo ships generated JSON evidence packs, deterministic evaluators, MCP tools, source hashes, runtime decision contracts, and pilot/value-model artifacts.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: MCP auth is a durable platform-control wedge.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The evaluator is deterministic and source-backed for the reference workflows.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Live OAuth metadata, tenant resource identifiers, client registration evidence, and gateway logs.
enterprise_questions.question: How does the project handle protected MCP servers and token risk?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: It exposes MCP authorization conformance evidence for resource indicators, token audience, token passthrough, session binding, scope drift, and protected-resource review.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Custom MCP safety is now part of ChatGPT Enterprise/Edu and API adoption.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The public pack proves review logic; live connector verification needs tenant deployment.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Tool schemas, action modes, egress paths, prompt-injection exposure, and write-action approvals from the customer's MCP servers.
enterprise_questions.question: What prevents custom MCP servers from becoming a data exfiltration path?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: SecurityRecipes combines connector trust, app intake, protocol conformance, context egress, tool risk, and run receipts to review what a server can access, request, log, or write.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Multi-agent interoperability creates a new trust-boundary market.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The evidence is ready for reference A2A and MCP review paths.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Customer Agent Cards, provider identities, authentication schemes, and handoff payload samples.
enterprise_questions.question: How are remote agents and A2A handoffs reviewed?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The broader evidence stack treats Agent Cards, protocol handoffs, context minimization, identity, egress, and receipts as boundary decisions before context or authority crosses agents.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Evidence retention and trace normalization are monetizable enterprise infrastructure.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Reference fields and receipt templates exist; hosted trace retention is not yet proven.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: OpenTelemetry traces, gateway decisions, approval records, verifier output, and redacted incident replay samples.
enterprise_questions.question: Can a buyer reconstruct what an agent saw and did?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The telemetry contract and run receipts bind agent, model, MCP, context, policy, egress, approval, verifier, incident, trace, and redaction evidence into a reconstructable run record.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Standards drift can become a recurring paid service.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Current primary-source coverage is generated and reviewable.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Customer-specific policy watchlists and alert routes.
enterprise_questions.question: How does the site prove it is current enough for enterprise review?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The source freshness watch scans buyer-facing profiles, verifies primary publisher coverage, reports review cadence, and hashes source-backed artifacts.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Critical infrastructure readiness is a high-trust enterprise wedge for frontier labs and security platforms.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Reference critical-infrastructure readiness is generated and source-backed; customer sector proof and operator approvals are still required.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Sector-specific safety cases, OT/clinical/finance owner approvals, run receipts, telemetry, and incident response rehearsal evidence.
enterprise_questions.question: Can the product support critical-infrastructure AI pilots without overclaiming safety or compliance?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The critical-infrastructure secure-context pack starts with read-only context, maps sector hazards, requires MCP authorization and egress allow decisions, and holds high-impact actions until operator approval, safety-case, risk acceptance, receipt, and severe-risk evidence exist.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: The project already knows which proof points unlock valuation.
enterprise_questions.current_state: ROI math is transparent and conservative, not a revenue claim.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Run count, reviewer time, avoided remediation hours, automation success rate, false-positive rate, safe holds, and renewal intent.
enterprise_questions.question: What proves ROI instead of assumed productivity?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The value model labels default economics as assumptions, while the pilot pack specifies the telemetry needed to replace those assumptions with customer runtime evidence.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Secure-by-design posture lowers enterprise adoption friction.
enterprise_questions.current_state: Reference controls are inspectable and can be mapped to customer governance.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Customer policy ownership, support SLAs, incident handling, and telemetry accountability.
enterprise_questions.question: How does this align with secure-by-design expectations?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The evidence stack makes secure defaults, deny-by-default gates, source transparency, customer outcome measurement, and executive-readable proof part of the product shape.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Open distribution can feed closed operational evidence and hosted controls.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The open-core boundary is explicitly documented.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Which hosted surfaces design partners will actually pay for and renew.
enterprise_questions.question: What stays open, and what becomes paid?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: Open artifacts include recipes, profiles, generators, evidence packs, local evaluators, and read-only MCP access; paid surfaces are hosted policy, private context, connector drift, receipts, eval replay, and trust-center APIs.

enterprise_questions.acquirer_signal: Clear path from open authority to enterprise control-plane value.
enterprise_questions.current_state: The acquisition thesis is shaped; commercial proof is still required.
enterprise_questions.customer_evidence_needed: Paid pilot or renewal signal, usage telemetry, support burden, integration depth, and buyer references.
enterprise_questions.question: What must be true before a $10-20M acquisition outcome is credible?
enterprise_questions.short_answer: The project needs three to five design partners, hosted auth, tenant isolation, live connector monitoring, signed receipts, customer-private evidence ingestion, metering, and renewal proof.

features_assessed.assessment: Strong strategic fit and partly represented by the existing marketplace, connector intake, connector trust, and tool-surface drift artifacts.

features_assessed.assessment: High-value product wedge, but the current repository should first package which trace fields and receipts buyers expect before implementing hosted storage.

features_assessed.assessment: Selected for this run because it turns existing technical depth into the concise review packet a frontier lab, VC, or enterprise buyer would ask for first.

industry_bets.monetizable_surface: Hosted MCP policy, connector inventory, protected-resource metadata checks, and trust-center exports.
industry_bets.securityrecipes_response: Expose authorization conformance, tool-surface drift, connector trust, and buyer diligence through MCP-readable evidence.
industry_bets.thesis: MCP becomes the default tool and data integration layer for agentic platforms, creating durable demand for authorization, connector trust, drift, and telemetry controls.

industry_bets.monetizable_surface: Signed Agent Card review, handoff replay, and delegated authority evidence.
industry_bets.securityrecipes_response: Bind A2A and MCP protocol conformance to handoff boundaries, egress policy, identity evidence, and receipts.
industry_bets.thesis: A2A-style agent discovery and delegation makes Agent Cards, authentication, authorization, and context minimization a new review boundary.

industry_bets.monetizable_surface: Trace normalization, receipt vault, compliance export, and SIEM/SOAR adapters.
industry_bets.securityrecipes_response: Package required trace fields, redaction states, policy hashes, receipt ids, and verifier outcomes as generated contracts.
industry_bets.thesis: OpenTelemetry MCP and GenAI conventions become the common substrate for reconstructing agent runs in enterprise observability and audit workflows.

industry_bets.monetizable_surface: Hosted control-plane policy, evidence APIs, source freshness, and executive posture reporting.
industry_bets.securityrecipes_response: Position SecurityRecipes as the secure context and evidence control plane between agents, tools, identity, telemetry, and governance.
industry_bets.thesis: CSA and enterprise buyers are framing autonomous agents as a control-plane problem, not only an LLM application problem.

intent: Define a buyer- and acquirer-facing diligence brief for SecurityRecipes so the secure context layer can be reviewed as a concise product, not a sprawling set of generated artifacts. The brief converts the current evidence packs, MCP tools, source freshness proof, pilot motion, and value model into enterprise questions, objection handling, and deal-room next steps.

objection_handlers.answer: The site now ships generated evidence packs, deterministic evaluators, MCP tools, source hashes, runtime decision contracts, pilot motions, and value-model artifacts. The remaining gap is hosted enforcement, not product definition.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Run a design-partner pilot that replaces assumptions with customer traces and receipts.
objection_handlers.objection: This is just a documentation site.

objection_handlers.answer: That is exactly why a source-fresh secure context layer matters: MCP authorization, OpenAI MCP safety guidance, A2A, OpenTelemetry MCP semantics, OWASP MCP, and CSA control-plane work are moving quickly enough to justify continuous evidence refresh.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Attach customer-specific standards watchlists and alert routes.
objection_handlers.objection: Agentic AI security standards are too early to productize.

objection_handlers.answer: Correct. The value model is intentionally assumption-based and the pilot pack names the telemetry required to prove renewal value. That honesty makes the diligence story more credible.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Secure three to five design partners and measure usage, saved reviewer time, safe holds, and renewal intent.
objection_handlers.objection: There is no recurring revenue proof.

objection_handlers.answer: MCP is becoming the enterprise integration layer for agents. SecurityRecipes does not assume MCP is safe; it wraps MCP authorization, tool risk, connector trust, protocol conformance, telemetry, and receipts around it.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Run live connector metadata and authorization checks in a tenant deployment.
objection_handlers.objection: MCP itself introduces risk, so why build around it?

objection_handlers.answer: The product thesis is not to eliminate prompt injection with one filter. It uses context trust, egress policy, app intake, MCP review, approval receipts, telemetry, and run receipts so risky actions are held, denied, or reconstructed.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Replay real prompt-injection scenarios against customer MCP tools and approval paths.
objection_handlers.objection: Prompt injection can still make agents take unintended actions.

objection_handlers.answer: Incumbents own logs, tickets, and identity, but SecurityRecipes already has a vendor-neutral taxonomy, generated controls, MCP-native evidence, and open distribution that can accelerate an incumbent roadmap or acquisition.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Demonstrate integrations that export receipt and telemetry evidence into existing security workflows.
objection_handlers.objection: Incumbent security platforms can add this.

objection_handlers.answer: It should not. The open layer defines the evidence shape, while the paid layer should ingest hashes, metadata-only telemetry, tenant-side context registrations, and customer-private evidence under customer controls.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Build private context registry and metadata-only telemetry ingestion for design partners.
objection_handlers.objection: The open repo cannot handle customer-private data.

objection_handlers.answer: The buyer diligence brief exists to solve that problem: it turns the artifact set into buyer briefs, RFP-style questions, objection handlers, and exact MCP tools to query first.
objection_handlers.next_proof: Use this brief as the first artifact in pilot and acquirer conversations.
objection_handlers.objection: There are too many packs for a buyer to review.

positioning.buyer: Frontier model labs, AI platform vendors, security platform vendors, regulated enterprises, VC diligence teams, and acquisition reviewers
positioning.category: Secure context buyer diligence brief for agentic AI and MCP governance
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes AI easier to approve by giving reviewers one current, MCP-readable brief that answers what is proven, what is assumption-based, which evidence exists, which customer telemetry is still needed, and which hosted wedges create enterprise value.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI security review is converging around MCP authorization, custom MCP safety, A2A discovery, agentic skills, non-human identity, OpenTelemetry-aligned traces, source provenance, and secure-by-design accountability. Buyers need a concise bridge from those trends to concrete product proof.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
source_references.why_it_matters: Defines the shared buyer vocabulary for autonomous agents that plan, act, delegate, and make decisions across complex workflows.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: Names MCP-specific risks such as token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, missing telemetry, shadow servers, and context over-sharing.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: The latest MCP authorization spec anchors protected MCP servers in OAuth 2.1, protected resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, and secure token handling.

source_references.name: Agent2Agent Protocol Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: A2A makes agent discovery, capability negotiation, task management, and enterprise-ready authentication, authorization, tracing, and monitoring part of the multi-agent review surface.

source_references.name: Building MCP Servers for ChatGPT and API Integrations
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenAI's MCP guidance highlights prompt-injection, excessive parameter, read/write action, server trust, and sensitive-data risks that enterprise buyers must review before connecting custom MCP servers.

source_references.name: Developer Mode and MCP Apps in ChatGPT
source_references.why_it_matters: ChatGPT Enterprise/Edu developer mode puts custom MCP app vetting, RBAC, risk warnings, write-action confirmations, and compliance visibility into the buyer conversation.

source_references.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for MCP
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP-specific spans and metrics are emerging as the common evidence language for tool calls, sessions, methods, protocol versions, transports, errors, prompts, and tools.

source_references.name: Securing the Agentic Control Plane
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA's 2026 agentic control plane work validates identity-first controls, runtime authorization, privilege governance, agent accountability, and assurance infrastructure as strategic enterprise categories.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: Supports governance, measurement, monitoring, provenance, incident response, and lifecycle risk management for generative AI systems.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Profile on Trustworthy AI in Critical Infrastructure Concept Note
source_references.why_it_matters: Validates critical infrastructure as a near-term buyer surface for trustworthy AI requirements across IT, OT, ICS, sector lifecycles, and AI supply chains.

source_references.name: NIST SP 800-218A Secure Software Development Practices for Generative AI
source_references.why_it_matters: Gives producers, integrators, and acquirers a secure software development profile for AI systems, which is the right evidence bar for enterprise-ready agent tooling.

source_references.name: Secure by Design
source_references.why_it_matters: Frames secure defaults, accountability, transparency, and measurable customer outcomes as business requirements for technology producers.
